original_hypothesis.title: FeeCollectable.setFeeParameters Validates Old State Instead of New Input
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setFeeParameters() checks `if (feeBips > BIPS)` instead of `if (_feeBips > BIPS)`. It validates the current state variable rather than the new input parameter. This allows setting feeBips to any uint16 value (up to 65535 = 655.35%) as long as the current value is valid. The first call always passes since initial feeBips is 0. Once set above BIPS, calculateFees() will cause underflow (userAmount = amountIn - feeAmount where feeAmount > amountIn) and subsequent setFeeParameters calls will permanently revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The variable name mismatch between `feeBips` (state) and `_feeBips` (parameter) is a classic copy-paste or naming error. The validation is demonstrably wrong - it protects the wrong variable.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Validation checks `feeBips > BIPS` (state variable) instead of `_feeBips > BIPS` (input parameter)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: if (feeBips > BIPS) { revert ErrInvalidFeeBips(); } followed by feeBips = _feeBips;
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: feeBips (old state) is validated instead of _feeBips (new input parameter)

analysis_questions: Which contracts inherit FeeCollectable and are deployed?
What is the current feeBips value in deployed instances?
What is the impact on calculateFees if feeBips > BIPS?
validation_requirements: Call setFeeParameters with _feeBips > 10000 when current feeBips <= 10000
Verify the call succeeds and feeBips is set to the invalid value
Test calculateFees with feeBips > 10000 to confirm underflow/DoS

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Fee operator calls setFeeParameters(collector, 20000). Since current feeBips is 0, the check (0 > 10000) is false, allowing the set. Now feeBips=20000 (200%). calculateFees computes feeAmount = amountIn * 20000 / 10000 = 2*amountIn, causing underflow in userAmount = amountIn - feeAmount. Any subsequent setFeeParameters call also reverts (20000 > 10000), permanently locking the broken fee.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: DoS of fee-related functionality; potential fund extraction if fee > 100% is processable
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users of contracts inheriting FeeCollectable
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Irrecoverable once set - no function can fix feeBips since validation blocks all future calls

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test validates input parameter vs state variable in setFeeParameters

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by reading feeBips state variable

validation_result.mechanism: wrong variable validated in fee setter
validation_result.impact_summary: setFeeParameters validates the old state variable `feeBips` instead of the new input `_feeBips`, allowing fees >100% to be set. Once set, calculateFees underflows and all subsequent setFeeParameters calls permanently revert.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: FeeCollectable.sol line 15 checks `if (feeBips > BIPS)` where `feeBips` is the current state variable (declared on line 11 as `uint16 public feeBips`), not the input parameter `_feeBips`. Since the default value of `feeBips` is 0, the first call to setFeeParameters will always pass regardless of the `_feeBips` value. An operator can set `_feeBips` to any uint16 value up to 65535 (655.35%). Once feeBips is set above BIPS (10000), two consequences follow: (1) calculateFees will revert because `userAmount = amountIn - feeAmount` underflows when `feeAmount = amountIn * feeBips / BIPS > amountIn`, and (2) any subsequent call to setFeeParameters will revert because the check `if (feeBips > BIPS)` now evaluates the invalid stored value as true. This creates an irrecoverable state.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code at FeeCollectable.sol lines 14-22 clearly shows `feeBips` (state variable, line 11) being checked instead of `_feeBips` (function parameter, line 14). The naming convention with underscore prefix for parameters vs no prefix for state variables makes the bug obvious. The calculateFees function at lines 24-27 performs unchecked subtraction under Solidity 0.8+ which will revert on underflow when feeBips > BIPS.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Line 15 checks `feeBips` (the current state variable) instead of `_feeBips` (the input parameter). This validates the old value, not the new one being set. Should be `if (_feeBips > BIPS)`.

When feeBips > BIPS, feeAmount exceeds amountIn, causing the subtraction `amountIn - feeAmount` to underflow and revert under Solidity 0.8+. This creates a permanent DoS for any contract using calculateFees.

calling_sequence: 1. Fee operator calls setFeeParameters(_feeCollector, 20000) with current feeBips = 0
2. Check: if (feeBips > BIPS) → if (0 > 10000) → false → passes
4. Any call to calculateFees: feeAmount = amountIn * 20000 / 10000 = 2*amountIn → underflow on userAmount = amountIn - feeAmount
5. Any subsequent setFeeParameters call: if (feeBips > BIPS) → if (20000 > 10000) → true → reverts with ErrInvalidFeeBips()
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The vulnerability path starts at FeeCollectable.sol:14 where the function accepts `_feeBips` as parameter. At line 15, the validation incorrectly checks the state variable `feeBips` (declared at line 11) instead of the parameter `_feeBips`. The state variable is then assigned at line 22 (`feeBips = _feeBips`), writing the unchecked value. The downstream impact hits calculateFees at line 25-26 where `feeAmount = (amountIn * feeBips) / BIPS` produces a value larger than amountIn, and line 26 `userAmount = amountIn - feeAmount` underflows.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts directly call setFeeParameters, but the bug exists in the abstract contract code and affects any inheriting contract.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is straightforward and requires only fee operator access: (1) Call setFeeParameters(collector, 20000) when feeBips is at its default of 0. The check `feeBips > BIPS` evaluates to `0 > 10000` which is false, so the call succeeds. (2) feeBips is now 20000. (3) calculateFees will revert for any non-zero amountIn because `amountIn * 20000 / 10000 > amountIn`. (4) Attempting to fix by calling setFeeParameters again will revert because `20000 > 10000` is true. The invalid state is permanent and irrecoverable.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. Any contract inheriting FeeCollectable that calls setFeeParameters with an invalid _feeBips value will: (1) permanently break calculateFees causing DoS for all fee-related operations, (2) permanently lock the invalid fee value since future setFeeParameters calls revert, and (3) require contract redeployment to fix. Even if the operator is trusted, an accidental misconfiguration (e.g., passing basis points as a percentage, like 5000 instead of 500) creates an irrecoverable state.
recommended_mitigations: Change line 15 from `if (feeBips > BIPS)` to `if (_feeBips > BIPS)` to validate the new input parameter instead of the old state variable
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a contract inheriting FeeCollectable with isFeeOperator returning true for test address. 2. Call setFeeParameters(someAddress, 20000). 3. Verify feeBips is now 20000. 4. Call calculateFees(1000) and observe revert due to underflow. 5. Call setFeeParameters(someAddress, 5000) and observe revert due to ErrInvalidFeeBips.
uncertainties: Which deployed contracts currently inherit FeeCollectable and have active fee parameters - this determines real-world impact scope
additional_testing_needed: Identify all contracts inheriting FeeCollectable in the deployed system
Check current feeBips values on deployed instances to assess if the bug has already been triggered
